title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
After a brief stop for food, Ellery Queen, District Attorney Escham, and Isam arrive in Arroyo to meet Sheriff Rudden. They visit the sheriff's office, located in a building near the town hall, where Ellery inquires about the whereabouts of "old Pete," a local man. Sheriff Rudden claims he has not seen Pete for two months and describes Pete's shack as being situated in a desolate, difficult-to-reach area in the mountains near a cave region.

The Sheriff leads the group away from the town hall in the Duesenberg. They drive toward the mountains and park the vehicle in a thicket, after which they begin a strenuous uphill climb through dense, sun-blocking jungle. They eventually reach a clearing containing a crude shack situated under a large granite outcropping. The shack is surrounded by a thirty-foot-high protective fence made of tangled barbed wire and lacks a standard entrance.

To ensure secrecy, Isam bribes the Sheriff, who agrees to act as if he has seen nothing. As Escham and Ellery approach the fence, a man—identified by Ellery as old Pete—points a rifle through a barbed-wire-protected window and threatens to shoot them. Isam shouts that they are police and presents a letter from Megalos to establish credibility. The rifle is lowered, and the disheveled, bearded old Pete opens the heavy, nail-studded door. Because there is no other way in, Pete instructs the men to climb over the barbed wire fence to enter.
